Freedom Forum, Nieman Foundation online

November 1994
Journalism & NewsroomTechnology

The Freedom Forum and Nieman Foundation have launched online presences through NandO.net, offering conference transcripts, reports, and multimedia content focused on public-interest journalism.

For those of you who haven't noticed yet, the Freedom Forum and the Nieman Foundation have gone online. Both organizations are available on the Web via NandO.net an Internet gateway operated by the Raleigh News & Observer.

The Nieman Foundation page (http://www.nando.net/may/confpage.biml) offers full transcripts of discussions at its recent conference dealing with the role and challenges of public-interest journalism in an expanding information marketplace.

The Freedom Forum page (http://www.nando.net/prof/freedom/1994/freedom.biml) features some of its published reports (including some color photographs and graphics) and selected transcripts of seminars and audio "sound bites."
